overview: Refactor the internal cluster service to reduce duplication across supported Camunda versions while preserving current topology behavior, explicitly review supported generated-client coverage, and prove completion with focused tests plus conditional documentation updates.

userStories.title: Stabilize the shared cluster contract
userStories.description: Lock the reviewed cluster service surface and baseline factory behavior so later refactoring happens against an explicit, tested contract within the existing package structure.
acceptanceCriteria: The cluster service entry point remains the single version-selection path in the existing package layout.
Factory tests cover supported-version selection and unknown-version failure behavior.
The reviewed shared service surface is aligned across the cluster API and both version-specific contracts.
userStories.notes: Reuse the current factory-plus-versioned-service pattern. Keep this slice limited to contract and baseline test stabilization before core refactoring.

userStories.title: Refactor topology handling without behavior change
userStories.description: Improve readability and reduce avoidable duplication in the shared cluster entry point and the v87/v88 topology implementations while preserving current observable topology and error behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared responsibilities between the factory, API surface, and version-specific services are clearer and duplication is reduced.
Existing topology-fetch behavior, including current error handling, remains unchanged from the caller perspective.
Updated v87 and v88 service tests pass with preserved success and failure-path coverage.
userStories.notes: Prefer small shared helpers or localized normalization improvements over new abstraction layers. Do not rename packages or change layout.

userStories.title: Complete supported-version capability review
userStories.description: Review supported generated cluster capabilities against the current service surface and either add one justified low-risk missing capability or record an explicit no-addition decision.
acceptanceCriteria: The supported generated cluster capabilities are explicitly reviewed against the service surface.
Any added capability fits the current boundaries and is covered by automated tests, or the no-addition decision is explicitly documented.
The final capability decision is traceable in project artifacts and does not introduce broad structural change.
userStories.notes: This story depends on the stabilized service surface. Treat the review as mandatory and the new capability as conditional.

userStories.title: Prove completion with integration validation and docs parity
userStories.description: Finalize the feature by updating integration coverage, making documentation impact explicit, and running the repository validation path required for safe merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration coverage validates the final cluster service behavior for supported versions.
Documentation impact is explicit: either no user-visible change is confirmed or affected docs are updated in the same change.
The final validation path includes targeted cluster verification and a successful `make test` run.
userStories.notes: Keep this slice focused on proof of completion. Update README and CLI docs only if a user-visible cluster workflow or output changes.
